name: dissect-btrfs

metadata.name: dissect.btrfs
metadata.summary: A Dissect module implementing a parser for the Btrfs file system, a commonly used Linux filesystem.

documentation:
# dissect.btrfs

A Dissect module implementing a parser for the Btrfs file system, a commonly used Linux filesystem. For more
information, please see [the documentation](https://docs.dissect.tools/en/latest/projects/dissect.btrfs/index.html).

## Requirements

This project is part of the Dissect framework and requires Python.

Information on the supported Python versions can be found in the Getting Started section of [the documentation](https://docs.dissect.tools/en/latest/index.html#getting-started).

## Installation

`dissect.btrfs` is available on [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/dissect.btrfs/).

```bash
pip install dissect.btrfs
```

This project decompresses lzo compressed file systems and can use the faster, native (C-based) lzo implementation when
installed, instead of the slower pure Python implementation provided by `dissect.util`. To use these faster
implementations, install the package with the lzo extra:

```bash
pip install "dissect.btrfs[lzo]"
```

Unfortunately there is no binary `python-lzo` wheel for PyPy installations on Windows, so it won't be installed there.

This module including the lzo extra is also automatically installed if you install the `dissect` package.

## Build and test instructions

This project uses `tox` to build source and wheel distributions. Run the following command from the root folder to build
these:

```bash
tox -e build
```

The build artifacts can be found in the `dist/` directory.

`tox` is also used to run linting and unit tests in a self-contained environment. To run both linting and unit tests
using the default installed Python version, run:

```bash
tox
```

For a more elaborate explanation on how to build and test the project, please see [the
documentation](https://docs.dissect.tools/en/latest/contributing/tooling.html).

## Contributing

The Dissect project encourages any contribution to the codebase. To make your contribution fit into the project, please
refer to [the development guide](https://docs.dissect.tools/en/latest/contributing/developing.html).

## Copyright and license

Dissect is released as open source by Fox-IT (<https://www.fox-it.com>) part of NCC Group Plc
(<https://www.nccgroup.com>).

Developed by the Dissect Team (<dissect@fox-it.com>) and made available at <https://github.com/fox-it/dissect>.

License terms: AGPL3 (<https://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl-3.0.html>). For more information, see the LICENSE file.

functions.name: dissect.btrfs.tree._cmp_key
documentation:
Compare a B-tree key on disk to a given object ID, type and offset.

Returns -1 if the key on disk is less, 0 if the key is equal and 1 if the key is greater.
Search parameters of `None` are ignored in the comparison.

Args:

: key: The key on disk to compare.
  objectid: Optional object ID to compare against.
  type: Optional type to compare against.
  offset: Optional offset to compare against.

classes.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.BTree

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.BTree.cursor
documentation: Return a new cursor into the B-tree.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.BTree.find
documentation:
Search for a single item in the B-tree.

Args:
    objectid: Optional object ID to search for.
    type: Optional type to search for.
    offset: Optional offset to search for.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.BTree._read_node
documentation:
Helper method for reading a node in the B-tree.

Args:
    address: The address of the node to read.

Represent a Btrfs B-tree.

One of `root_item` or `root_offset` must be given.

Args:

: btrfs: The filesystem this B-tree belongs to.
  root_item: Optional `btrfs_root_item` to open the B-tree from.
  root_offset: Optional offset to open the B-tree from.

classes.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.Cursor

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.Cursor.reset
documentation: Reset the cursor.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.Cursor.push
documentation:
Push the cursor down a node.

Args:

: address: The address of the node to push down to.
  initial_index: The initial index to seek to in the new node. `-1` means the last item.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.Cursor.pop
documentation: Pop up a node.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.Cursor._read_key
documentation:
Read a key at the specified index.

Args:
    index: The index of the key to read.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.Cursor._read_item
documentation:
Read an item at the specified index.

Args:
    index: The index of the item to read.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.Cursor.next
documentation:
Traverse to the next leaf item.

Will move up and down the tree to find the next leaf item.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.Cursor.next_node
documentation:
Traverse to the next node.

Will move up and down the tree to find the next node.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.Cursor.prev
documentation:
Traverse to the previous leaf item.

Will move up and down the tree to find the previous leaf item.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.Cursor.prev_node
documentation:
Traverse to the previous leaf item.

Will move up and down the tree to find the previous leaf item.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.Cursor.first
documentation: Move the cursor to the first leaf item.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.Cursor.last
documentation: Move the cursor to the last leaf item.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.Cursor.get
documentation:
Retrieve the leaf item and the associated data at the current cursor position.

Cursor must be positioned at a leaf item.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.Cursor.item
documentation:
Retrieve a leaf or branch item.

Cursor can be positioned at a branch or leaf item.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.Cursor.items
documentation: Iterate over all items in the current node.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.Cursor.data
documentation: Return the associated data of the current leaf item.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.Cursor.iter
documentation:
Search and iterate the B-tree for the specified key.

Stop iterating if the current item no longer matches the given parameters.

Args:

: objectid: Optional object ID to search for.
  type: Optional type to search for.
  offset: Optional offset to search for.
  ignore_offset: Only use the `offset` argument for the initial positioning of the cursor,

  > but ignore it for future iterations. This is useful for e.g. iterating file extents.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.Cursor.walk
documentation:
Walk all leaf items of the B-tree and yield all matching leafs.

Args:
    objectid: Optional object ID to yield items of.
    type: Optional type to yield items of.
    offset: Optional offset to yield items of.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.tree.Cursor.search
documentation:
Perform a binary search on the current node for the key with the given parameters.

Puts the cursor at the index of the matching item, or just before a "greater" item if no exact match is found.
`True` is returned if this is the case, else `False` is returned and the cursor position is reset.

Args:

: objectid: Optional object ID to search for.
  type: Optional type to search for.
  offset: Optional offset to search for.

A basic cursor implementation for interacting with a {class}`BTree`.

Args:

: btree: The {class}`BTree` to open a cursor on.

functions.name: dissect.btrfs.stream.decode_extent
documentation:
Decode a compressed extent.

Args:
    buf: The extent data to decode.
    compression: The compression type to decompress from.
    encryption: The encryption type - currently unused.
    sector_size: The sector size of the filesystem, necessary for LZO decompression.

classes.name: dissect.btrfs.stream.ChunkStream

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.stream.ChunkStream.add
documentation:
Add a chunk to the stream.

This will iterate all stripes and link them to the appropriate devices.
Allows for missing devices, if the RAID profile allows this.

Args:
    offset: The logical offset to add this chunk for.
    chunk: The chunk item to add.

Implements a stream over Btrfs chunks, including basic RAID support.

Args:
    btrfs: The filesystem this stream belongs to.

classes.name: dissect.btrfs.stream.ExtentStream
documentation:
Implement a stream over Btrfs file extents.

Supports compression.

Args:

: fh: The file-like object to read the data from, usually the {class}`ChunkStream` of the filesystem.
  extents: The list of extents to open the stream on.
  sector_size: The sector size of the filesystem, necessary for LZO decompression.

functions.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs._parse_ts
documentation: Parse a Btrfs time specification into a nanosecond timestamp.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.Btrfs.get
documentation:
Retrieve a Btrfs inode by path or inode number.

Args:
    path: Filesystem path or inode number.
    node: Optional inode used for relative lookups.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.Btrfs.subvolumes
documentation: Yield all subvolumes.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.Btrfs.find_subvolume
documentation:
Find a subvolume by path.

Args:
    path: The path of the subvolume to find.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.Btrfs.open_subvolume
documentation:
Open a subvolume.

Args:
    objectid: The objectid of the subvolume to open.
    parent: Optional parent node to attach to the root of the subvolume.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.Btrfs._initialize_chunks
documentation: Initialize the logical data stream by reading the system chunk array and traversing the chunk tree.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.Btrfs._open_tree
documentation:
Open a tree by object ID.

Args:
    objectid: The object ID of the tree to open.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.Btrfs._open_default_subvolume
documentation: Find and open the subvolume that's configured as default.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.Btrfs._read_node
documentation:
Helper method for reading a node.

Args:
    address: The node address to read.

Btrfs filesystem implementation.

This implementation supports most basic Btrfs features such as subvolumes, compression and (meta)data RAID.
To open a RAID volume, simply pass all file-like objects that belong to the RAID set as a list.

Args:
    fh: A file-like object for the volume to use for parsing Btrfs.

classes.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.Subvolume

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.Subvolume.get
documentation:
Retrieve a Btrfs inode by path or inode number.

Args:
    path: Filesystem path or inode number.
    node: Optional inode used for relative lookups.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.Subvolume.inode
documentation: Return an {class}`INode` by number, optionally attaching a type and parent.

Represent a Btrfs subvolume.

Btrfs has support for multiple subvolumes. The default subvolume is the `FS_TREE` subvolume.
Each subvolume has its own B-tree.

Args:

: btrfs: The filesystem this subvolume belongs to.
  objectid: The object ID of the subvolume to open.
  parent: Optional parent node to attach to the root of the subvolume.

classes.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode

documentation: Return the parsed inode structure.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode.otime_ns

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode.is_dir
documentation: Return whether this inode is a directory.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode.is_file
documentation: Return whether this inode is a regular file.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode.is_symlink
documentation: Return whether this inode is a symlink.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode.is_block_device
documentation: Return whether this inode is a block device.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode.is_character_device
documentation: Return whether this inode is a character device.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode.is_device
documentation: Return whether this inode is a device.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode.is_fifo
documentation: Return whether this inode is a FIFO file.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode.is_socket
documentation: Return whether this inode is a socket file.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode.is_ipc
documentation: Return whether this inode is an IPC file.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode.link
documentation: Return the symlink target.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode.link_inode
documentation: Resolve the symlink target to an inode.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode.path
documentation: Return the path to this inode within the subvolume. In case of multiple hardlinks, return the first path.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode.full_path
documentation: Return the full path to this inode. In case of multiple hardlinks, return the first path.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode.get
documentation:
Retrieve a Btrfs inode relative from this inode.

Args:
    path: Filesystem path.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode.paths
documentation:
Yield all paths (hardlinks) to this inode.

By default only resolves up to the root of the subvolume this inode belongs to.
For a full path to the root of the filesystem tree, set `full` to `True`.

Args:

: full: Whether to fully resolve the path up to the root of the filesystem tree.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode.listdir
documentation: Return a directory listing.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode.iterdir
documentation: Iterate directory contents.

methods.name: dissect.btrfs.btrfs.INode.open
documentation:
Return the data stream for the inode.

File data in Btrfs can be inlined in the B-tree or stored in file extents. In both cases it can be compressed.

Represent a Btrfs inode.

Args:
    subvolume: The subvolume this inode belongs to.
    inum: The inode number of this inode.
    type: Optional file type of this inode, as observed in a directory entry.
    parent: Optional parent of this inode, if this inode is parsed from a directory listing.
